content.claim: A 2025 Budapest case shows that central district-level school-merger decisions can be reversed after local mobilization (4,000-signature petition and two demonstrations), when a district centre withdrew its reorganization proposal.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: Urban case, not rural; enrollment-decline context differs from depopulation context
Single reversal does not establish generalizable pattern
No data on reversal success rate across other contested merger proposals
Does not show whether reversals are permanent or merely delayed
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
